children.name: Corythaeola
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the largest of all turacos, a striking blue-grey bird with a tall, flattened black-and-blue crest. It is a highly social, frugivorous species of Central African forests.

children.name: Corythaeola cristata
children.description: The largest turaco, measuring up to 76 centimetres in length with a majestic blue-grey plumage, a tall black-tipped crest and a bright yellow bill and red-tipped tail. It inhabits the rainforest canopy of equatorial Africa from Guinea to Kenya. It moves in noisy flocks through the treetops, flapping lazily with deep, slow wingbeats.

children.name: Tauraco
children.description: Vividly coloured turacos with iridescent green upperparts, crimson flight feathers and a distinctive white or pale patch behind the eye. They are among the most brilliantly pigmented birds, owing their green colour to the unique copper-based pigment turacoverdin.

children.name: Tauraco corythaix
children.description: Endemic to the coastal and montane forests of South Africa, Swaziland and Mozambique, this brilliant green turaco sports a white-tipped crest and a crimson patch on the wing. It is a noisy but elusive inhabitant of the canopy, often betraying its presence with a loud, raucous 'kraak-kraak-kraak' as it bounds through the branches.

children.name: Tauraco livingstonii
children.description: Found in the lowland and montane forests of eastern Africa from Tanzania to South Africa, this species closely resembles the Knysna Turaco but has a taller, more pointed crest tipped with white. Its flight is direct, with rapid wingbeats interspersed with glides that show off the brilliant crimson flight feathers.

children.name: Tauraco leucotis
children.description: Distinguished by the large white patch on the side of the face and its green crest tipped with white, this turaco inhabits the highlands of Ethiopia and Eritrea. It prefers juniper and podocarp forests at elevations above 1,500 metres. Its deep, guttural croaking calls echo through the misty montane woodlands.

children.name: Tauraco schuettii
children.description: A medium-sized green turaco with a black bill, a white-tipped crest, and a distinctive white patch behind the eye, found in lowland and montane forests of Central and East Africa. It inhabits the forest canopy, feeding on fruits and berries. Its loud, raucous calls are a common sound in the African rainforest.

children.name: Tauraco leucolophus
children.description: A striking turaco with a prominent white crest, green body, and crimson flight feathers, found in lowland forests of West and Central Africa from Nigeria to the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It is a social, arboreal frugivore that travels in small, noisy flocks through the canopy.

children.name: Tauraco fischeri
children.description: A green turaco with a distinctive red bill, a white-tipped crest, and a red patch of bare skin around the eye, found in the coastal forests of East Africa from Somalia to Tanzania. It is threatened by habitat loss and is restricted to remaining fragments of lowland forest.

children.name: Tauraco hartlaubi
children.description: A beautiful green turaco with a white-tipped crest, a white patch behind the eye, and a yellow bill, found in the montane forests of Kenya and Tanzania. It is common in the highland forests of Mount Kenya and the Aberdare Range. Its loud, resonant calls are a characteristic sound of the East African highlands.

children.name: Tauraco macrorhynchus
children.description: A large green turaco with a prominent yellow bill, a white-tipped crest, and crimson flight feathers, found in lowland and montane forests of West and Central Africa from Sierra Leone to Angola. It is a frugivorous canopy dweller, often seen clambering through the branches in small groups.

children.name: Tauraco bannermani
children.description: A critically endangered turaco restricted to the highland forests of the Bamenda Highlands in western Cameroon. It has a green body, a white-tipped crest, a red bill, and a distinctive red patch around the eye. Its population is estimated at fewer than 2,500 individuals and is declining due to deforestation.

children.name: Tauraco persa
children.description: A vivid green turaco with a green-tipped white crest, a white line below the eye, and a red bill, found in lowland forests of West and Central Africa. It is a common and conspicuous canopy frugivore, often seen bounding through the branches with its noisy, raucous calls.

children.name: Crinifer
children.description: A genus of grey, brown, or white turacos with distinctive shaggy crests and long tails, found in open woodlands and savannas of Africa. Unlike the forest-dwelling green turacos, they inhabit drier habitats and are named for their raucous 'go-away' calls.

children.name: Crinifer personatus
children.description: A grey turaco with a distinctive bare black face, a shaggy crest, and a long tail, found in savannas and woodlands of eastern Africa from Ethiopia to Mozambique. Its loud, nasal 'go-away' call is one of the most familiar sounds of the African bush.

children.name: Crinifer leucogaster
children.description: A medium-sized turaco with grey upperparts, a white belly, a shaggy crest, and a distinctive white patch on the wing, found in dry savannas and woodlands of eastern Africa. Its raucous, grating calls are a characteristic sound of the arid bush.

children.name: Crinifer zonurus
children.description: A large, heavy-bodied turaco with grey-brown plumage, a shaggy crest, a yellow bill, and a long, graduated tail, found in woodlands and savannas of eastern Africa from Ethiopia to Tanzania. It feeds primarily on fruits and is often seen clambering clumsily through the branches.

children.name: Crinifer piscator
children.description: A large, grey-brown turaco with a shaggy crest, a thick yellow bill, and a long, graduated tail, found in savannas and woodlands of West Africa from Senegal to Cameroon. It is a noisy, conspicuous bird that travels in small flocks, its raucous calls echoing across the savanna.

children.name: Crinifer concolor
children.description: A uniform grey turaco with a shaggy crest, a black bill, and a long tail, found in savannas and woodlands of southern Africa. Its distinctive, nasal 'kweh' call sounds like 'go-away', giving the group its onomatopoeic common name. It is a common and conspicuous bird in the bushveld.

children.name: Musophaga
children.description: Deep violet-blue turacos with a vivid yellow or orange forehead and a heavy, domed bill. They are adapted to open woodlands and savannas rather than dense rainforest.

children.name: Musophaga violacea
children.description: A stunning deep violet-blue turaco with a bright yellow forehead and a heavy red-and-yellow bill, found across West Africa from Senegal to Nigeria. It prefers riverine forests and moist savanna woodlands. Despite its brilliant coloration, it is surprisingly well camouflaged among the dappled shadows of the canopy.

children.name: Musophaga rossae
children.description: Ranging from Cameroon and Angola to Kenya and Botswana, this deep violet turaco replaces the Violet Turaco in central and eastern Africa. It has a striking yellow bill and a prominent red-orange patch around the eye. It is an active, gregarious bird that travels in small flocks, clambering through the branches in search of fruits.

children.name: Gallirex
children.description: A small genus of turacos with a distinctive purple crest and a glossy green body, occupying the woodlands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Gallirex porphyreolophus
children.description: A striking turaco with a glossy purple crest, iridescent green body and crimson wings, found from Uganda and Kenya to South Africa. It is a common inhabitant of riverine and coastal forests as well as well-wooded gardens. Its loud, resonant 'go-away' call is a familiar sound in the bushveld.

children.name: GALLIREX
children.description: GALLIREX — a genus of turacos.

children.name: GALLIREX aurantiaca
children.description: A medium-sized turaco with vibrant orange feathers on its wings and a distinctive call.

children.name: CORYTHAEOLA kiboko
children.description: A large turaco with a distinctive red crown and a loud, resonant call.

children.name: CRINIFER tanae
children.description: A small turaco with a grey back and white underside, found in the Tana River region.

children.name: MUSOPHAGA zoea
children.description: A medium-sized turaco with a distinctive black stripe on its neck and a loud, chirping call.
